title: scrophularia suffruticans
intro: Scrophularia suffruticans, also known as the small figwort or small scorpion plant, is a perennial herb belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Scrophularia suffruticans include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Scrophularia suffruticans can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
